operating_state: Total company net sales increased 2% to $84.0 billion driven by 4% organic growth, with pricing actions of 3-4% partially offset by 2% unfavorable foreign exchange impacts. Health Care delivered growth led by oral care innovation, Fabric & Home Care and Grooming achieved solid growth despite competitive pressures, while Beauty faced headwinds from super-premium brand decline. Core earnings per share grew 12% to $6.59 despite reported EPS increasing only 2% due to impairment charges.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 350 basis points to 51.4% driven by manufacturing productivity savings, lower commodity costs, and pricing realization, partially offset by unfavorable product mix and foreign exchange impacts. SG&A expenses increased 200 basis points to 27.7% of sales primarily due to higher marketing investments and wage inflation despite productivity savings benefits.
material_events: The company recorded $1.3 billion non-cash impairment charge on Gillette intangible asset due to higher discount rates and currency weakening. Substantially liquidated operations in Nigeria with accumulated currency translation losses and completed Argentina divestiture, expecting additional charges in Q1 FY2025. Executed share repurchase program and maintained quarterly dividend while completing euro-denominated debt offerings.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange volatility intensified with strengthening dollar creating translation headwinds across international markets representing over half of net sales, reducing net earnings by approximately $589 million. Currency devaluation pressures particularly affected Enterprise Markets with operations liquidated in Nigeria and Argentina due to challenging macroeconomic conditions. Geopolitical tensions create supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures.
forward_outlook: Management maintains long-term growth algorithm targeting organic sales growth above market rates, Core EPS growth of mid-to-high single digits, and adjusted free cash flow productivity of 90% or greater. The company expects disciplined investment approach during macroeconomic pressures while delivering superior shareholder returns through continued innovation investments and productivity initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: The Gillette intangible asset remains susceptible to future impairment risk from adverse macroeconomic changes, foreign currency devaluation, or market contraction. Multiple tax audits are in process across numerous jurisdictions with open years ranging from 2010 forward. Pension and postretirement benefit obligations carry sensitivity to discount rate changes with potential material impacts on annual expenses.
